name: lucio
val_bpb_note: Best ablation mode (bigram: LeakyReLU + BigramHash2048, no JEPA), 688 steps, 600s wallclock, RTX 5060 Ti. Pre-quantization. Roundtrip int6+lzma: 1.7594. Full ablation table in README.
description: JEPA v2: diagnoses why same-sequence next-k JEPA collapses in causal LMs (geometric root cause, not hyperparameter issue) and provides a full ablation suite. Also implements int6+LZMA compression, BigramHash(2048) embedding, artifact EMA, and LeakyReLU(0.5)^2.
bugs_diagnosed_in_v1_jepa: EMA momentum 0.996 with <700 steps: target encoder barely diverges (1.72% weight update), JEPA task trivially easy
Single-step prediction (z[t]->z[t+1]) is redundant with CE objective for causal LMs
z_target computed from micro_batch[0] but paired with all 8 micro-steps (7/8 steps use mismatched batches)
key_finding: JEPA collapse persists even after fixing all three bugs. Root cause: consecutive positions in a causal LM share almost all context, making normalized representations inherently collinear. same-sequence next-k JEPA is near-zero MSE by construction, independent of momentum, prediction horizon, or training stage. Fix requires masking (I-JEPA style) or cross-sequence targets.
jepa_overhead: JEPA doubles step time (870ms -> 1481ms), halving steps in 600s budget (689 -> 406). Net effect: +0.154 BPB vs leaky baseline.
